policy.chosen_approach: COMBINATION, weighted to APPROACH 1 (INLINE-EXEMPT) for token home + the non-negotiable RCF floor fix, with APPROACH 3's ref-class TAG adopted as the *advisory* coverage-honesty layer. Reject APPROACH 2 (TEMPLATE-HOME) as the primary surface — a fourth skill-surface class is unjustified weight for a ~7-line vocabulary problem, and its own risk list concedes the lighter variant (tag an existing ref) is preferable unless a true fill-in template earns its keep; nothing here does. The decisive grounded fact that orders these: in build-skill-execution-observation.mjs line 666 `outcome = findings.length > 0 ? 'failed' : 'passed'`, and `findings` (line 662-665) come ONLY from requiredCommandFragments + requiredSummaryFragments. coverage (line 668-671, 737) is a *reported string that never gates outcome*. So the 'coverage floor that pulls reading IN' in the contradiction is ALREADY advisory — it cannot fail a run. The real re-read teeth is requiredCommandFragments=['verification-ladder.md'] (impl spec.json line 9-11), which the command-log matcher (line 663, fragmentFindings 615-632) satisfies only when collectOpenedBasenames/parseReadCommandBasenames sees the doc literally opened. That is the one thing forcing a wasteful re-read of intent the run already has. Fix THAT and the contradiction dissolves; everything else is honesty polish.
policy.policy: Single coherent policy, three coordinated moves, each grounded. MOVE A — TOKEN HOME (Approach 1): emittable load-bearing tokens come home to SKILL.md under a new headroom-exempt H2 `## Closeout Vocabulary`; create-skill's 'move schemas, examples, and theory into references/' rule is amended so ONLY prose/why/examples exile to references/, never literal strings a closeout must emit verbatim. MOVE B — HEADROOM (Approach 1): add 'Closeout Vocabulary' to PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS (check_skill_surface_preflight.py line 30) so the block is invisible to _core_nonempty_lines (106-108) and the commit-boundary ratchet evaluate_core_headroom (162-191), exactly as 'Load-Bearing Anchors'/'References' already are. MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 (line 18) + NEAR_CAP_WARN 195 (line 23) STILL count the real bytes — correct: the total ceiling is a file-size guard, core-nonempty is the decision-prose-density guard; only the latter created exile pressure. MOVE C — FLOOR (non-negotiable real teeth): in impl spec.json drop 'verification-ladder.md' from requiredCommandFragments and assert the emittable TOKENS via requiredSummaryFragments instead, checking the actual claim ('did the run emit honest categorized closeout vocabulary' against finalAssistantText line 644/664) rather than the proxy ('did the run re-open the doc'). Adopt Approach 3's per-ref classTag (DUP/INLINE/DEPTH) as ADVISORY-only honesty on the coverage line so the reported denominator stops counting a not-re-opened ref as 'missing' — classTag never touches outcome (cosmetic by construction, since coverage is already advisory). Headroom gate and coverage measurement are NOT in genuine conflict; the conflict was an artifact of RCF pinned to a ref-filename, which Move C removes — creating no new gate conflict.
policy.token_home_rule: A token is INLINE-in-core (SKILL.md `## Closeout Vocabulary` H2, headroom-exempt) iff a representative run must reproduce it VERBATIM to produce a well-formed or validator-passing closeout — i.e. a validator would substring-match it (requiredSummaryFragments / requiredCommandFragments) OR a closeout line is malformed without the literal string. Author decision test: 'Would a claim-fidelity spec substring-match this, or would a closeout line be malformed without the exact characters?' Yes -> inline token under Closeout Vocabulary. A paragraph teaching WHEN/WHY -> prose, stays in DEPTH references/. Concretely for impl: the Lint Gate status enum (ran-pass / ran-fail-fixed <command> / ran-fail-deferred <command> <issue|anchor> / not-detected / skipped <reason>) and the five completion-report category labels (durable / external-writes / test-only / verification / unverified-future) move from references/verification-ladder.md into impl's `## Closeout Vocabulary` as a terse label+one-clause list, NO worked examples. verification-ladder.md KEEPS all surrounding prose (per-surface ladder, browser/runtime/provider-API depth, why-these-categories rationale, capability-seeking rule) and is reduced to a pure pointer-to-core for the token list — never a parallel definition (kills the drift risk). The Output Shape field names already inline in impl SKILL.md (Implemented / Capability Delivered / Lint Gate / Verification / Unverified future behavior) stay inline as the canonical example this rule generalizes. Where a NEW token goes is now unambiguous: emittable-verbatim -> Closeout Vocabulary core; teaching prose -> DEPTH ref.
policy.headroom_change: EXEMPTION, not a cap raise. In scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py add 'Closeout Vocabulary' to PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS (line 30): `{"Load-Bearing Anchors", "References", "Closeout Vocabulary"}`. _remove_pressure_exempt_sections (91-103) strips the block before _core_nonempty_lines (106-108) tallies, so the inline token block is invisible to: (a) the build_report core_nonempty panel (line 359), (b) the commit-boundary ratchet scan_changed_skill_md -> evaluate_core_headroom (194-215 / 162-191) and its 'Move prose into references/' remediation message (~line 230), (c) any broad-gate test sharing CORE_NONEMPTY_HEADROOM_BUFFER (line 29). Net: moving impl's ~7 vocab lines from a ref INTO core costs ZERO core-nonempty headroom. UNCHANGED on purpose: MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 (18) and NEAR_CAP_WARN_LINES=195 (23) still count the real bytes — correct and load-bearing. Anti-abuse guard: skill_ergonomics / a preflight assertion must flag a Closeout Vocabulary block whose lines are multi-sentence prose or exceed ~12 lines, so authors cannot relabel ordinary decision prose to dodge the density gate.
policy.floor_change: PART 1 — real teeth (impl spec.json + claim_fidelity_lib.py): drop 'verification-ladder.md' from requiredCommandFragments; move the assertion to requiredSummaryFragments carrying a representative closeout token the pinned slice forces (e.g. a Lint Gate status like 'ran-pass'/'not-detected' plus a completion-report category like 'unverified-future' — chosen from the slice's actual forced closeout, re-baselined by a fresh capture, NOT assumed). This satisfies the claim against finalAssistantText (build-skill-execution-observation.mjs 644/664/666) WITHOUT a re-read, since the token is now always-loaded core. Relax the non-empty-RCF guard in claim_fidelity_lib.py (_validate_string_list line 57-62, invoked at line 137 for requiredCommandFragments): change from 'RCF must be non-empty' to 'at least one of requiredCommandFragments / requiredSummaryFragments non-empty' (RCF-or-RSF claim-channel guard) — NOT dropping the non-empty check entirely, which would let a spec assert nothing checkable. The RCF-must-be-engage-always invariant (137-140) is preserved for any RCF that remains. PART 2 — coverage honesty (advisory, build-skill-execution-observation.mjs 658-660/668-671/737 + spec referenceEngagement): adopt per-ref classTag DUP|INLINE|DEPTH beside engagement/rationale/claimRole. Compute the REPORTED coverage denominator over DEPTH-tagged refs the run's trigger reached; report DUP/INLINE refs on a separate advisory line, never in the floor ratio. Preserve the full declared list as report.coverage.declaredAll for backward-compat (run_skill_efficiency_ab.py and .test.mjs lines 217-330/737 consume coverage.declared — migrate them). classTag is additive-optional in _validate_engagement (one-of-three when present, tolerant default so untagged specs pass). impl spec.json: verification-ladder.md -> classTag DEPTH ('closeout vocab now inline; DEPTH only for per-surface ladder prose'); the four skill-inlined refs -> classTag INLINE (matching existing claimRole skill-inlined-context). CRITICAL: because outcome is findings-only (line 666), the classTag/denominator change is cosmetic-honesty — it stops the coverage LINE reading 'missing: verification-ladder.md' as a defect but is NOT what relieves the floor. Relief is 100% from Part 1 (moving the claim off RCF=ref-filename). A reviewer who edits only Part 2 leaves the wasteful re-read pressure intact.
policy.ref_class_tagging: Three-value classTag per-ref in spec.referenceEngagement (additive beside engagement/rationale/claimRole; the methodology contract at charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-...md line 120-121 already permits claimRole as 'another local note used only by the harness', so this slots in as a sharpening, not an overturn). DUP = a ref whose load-bearing tokens now duplicate core (e.g. a ref left as pure pointer-to-core after vocab promotion) — a faithful run must NOT re-read it; coverage must NEVER score a DUP miss. INLINE = the existing claimRole 'skill-inlined-context' population (impl's contract-consumption / design-lenses / review-gate / sequence-discipline): gist in core, doc opened only on a narrower trigger; engage-always for the concern but never a coverage floor. DEPTH = unique content a representative run is genuinely forced to open at its trigger — the ONLY legitimate coverage-denominator population. Validation: claim_fidelity_lib._validate_engagement (line 42-54) accepts classTag optionally (one of DUP/INLINE/DEPTH when present), and should require a rationale sentence on any DUP tag (it cannot judge truthfulness — the honest soft spot of any tag-based measurement). Surface the tag in quality catalog.yaml + index.md routing lines so a maintainer sees DUP/INLINE/DEPTH. The taxonomy MUST stay in sync across mutate->sync->verify (spec field, lib validator, .mjs consumer, catalog) — a partial edit is exactly the generated-surface drift the phase rules warn against.

policy.why_over_alternatives: Over APPROACH 2 (TEMPLATE-HOME): a fourth skill-surface class (templates/) burdens create-skill, catalog.yaml schema, find-skills, and the planner for a ~7-line vocabulary problem; its own risk list concedes the lighter variant (tag an existing ref) is preferable 'unless a true fill-in template earns its keep' — nothing here does. Worse, its floor relies on requiredCommandFragments matching the template basename 'anywhere in the command log', which a bare `ls templates/` or grep falsely satisfies — strictly WEAKER than today's 'opened verification-ladder.md'. Approach 1's inline-emit makes the token reproducible from always-loaded context (genuinely cheap) instead of requiring an open-and-fill ceremony. Over APPROACH 3 ALONE (MEASUREMENT-ONLY): it correctly identifies coverage is already advisory (line 666) — which is why I adopt its classTag — but it FREEZES token home in references and 'resolves' the contradiction by re-labeling rather than reducing reads. Its own risk #3 admits it does nothing when the token is needed BEFORE any trigger fires; risk #5 concedes it 'renamed the problem' without making the run cheaper; risk #6 admits classTag is cosmetic teeth (cannot stop any failure because outcome is findings-only), so on its own it leaves RCF=ref-filename re-read pressure fully intact. The genuine relief MUST come from moving the claim off requiredCommandFragments (my Move C Part 1), which Approach 3 does not do. So I take Approach 3's honest-denominator insight as advisory polish and Approach 1's inline-home + RCF-rewrite as the actual fix. Combined: token home is unambiguous (emittable-verbatim -> core, prose -> DEPTH ref), the headroom exemption is surgical (density gate relieved, size guard kept honest), the floor checks the real claim (emitted vocabulary) instead of a re-read proxy, and no NEW gate conflict is created (the total-ceiling guard is intentionally left to bite). DISSENT WORTH KEEPING: (1) MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 is intentionally NOT exempted and impl is at 190 — if reviewers trim load-bearing DECISION prose to make room for vocab, the cure reintroduces the disease at the total-ceiling layer; the fix is trimming true redundancy, never exempting the total. (2) classTag is cosmetic teeth — the PR must state in bold that Move C Part 1 (RCF->RSF) is load-bearing and Part 2 is honesty-only, or a reviewer under-edits the spec. (3) impl spec.json prompt (line 8) was authored to FORCE verification-ladder.md open; moving the claim to a summary token means a run could emit the token from core WITHOUT real categorized closeout work — re-verify the pinned slice's representativeness with a fresh Cautilus capture (ask-before-run per the cautilus on-demand contract), do NOT assume. (4) create-skill's propagation rule means every evaluator-required skill may have stranded vocab; land impl + the policy/validator/headroom plumbing as the proving slice and FILE AN ISSUE to sweep the rest — do not scope-creep the sweep into this change.

proposals.approach: INLINE-EXEMPT: carve load-bearing closeout/output tokens (enums, exact validator-checked strings, template field names) OUT of the headroom budget and INTO a tagged block in SKILL.md, so 'lean core' stops meaning 'tokens exiled to refs'. The headroom gate already has the exemption seam I need (`PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS = {'Load-Bearing Anchors', 'References'}` in scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py line 30) — I extend it to a new exempt H2 `## Closeout Vocabulary`. Prose elaboration (the *why*, the worked examples, the per-surface ladder) stays in DEPTH refs or is deleted; only the emittable tokens come home. The contradiction dissolves because the run can emit `ran-pass / not-detected / durable / unverified-future` straight from the always-loaded core, and the coverage/claim floor no longer forces a wasteful re-read of verification-ladder.md just to surface a vocabulary token it already had in context.
proposals.policy_summary: Two coupled rules, both grounded in the read files. (1) TOKEN_HOME rule in skills/public/create-skill/SKILL.md (today line 97-104 says 'SKILL.md contains trigger contract and decision skeleton only ... move schemas, examples, and theory into references/' — the exact rule that exiles vocab). Amend it: load-bearing tokens a representative run must EMIT to close out (closeout-status enums, completion-report category names, Output-Shape field labels, any string a claim-fidelity spec lists in requiredCommandFragments/requiredSummaryFragments) live INLINE in SKILL.md under a single tagged `## Closeout Vocabulary` H2; ONLY the prose that explains/justifies/exemplifies them moves to references/. The test for 'is this a token vs prose': would a validator substring-match it, or would a closeout line be malformed without the literal string? If yes -> inline token. If it is a paragraph of rationale -> ref. (2) HEADROOM rule in scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py: add `'Closeout Vocabulary'` to PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS so the token block does not count against MAX_CORE_NONEMPTY_LINES (160) / the CORE_NONEMPTY_HEADROOM_BUFFER (4) ratchet, exactly as `Load-Bearing Anchors` and `References` are already exempt. This makes the budget measure decision-prose density (what it is actually for) instead of penalizing the irreducible vocabulary surface. The MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 total ceiling still applies (the block is real bytes), but the core-nonempty ratchet that drives the 'move prose into references/' message (line 229-232) stops firing on vocabulary.
proposals.token_home: Load-bearing tokens belong INLINE in SKILL.md under a clearly-tagged `## Closeout Vocabulary` H2 block (the headroom-exempt section). Concretely for impl: the Lint Gate status enum (`ran-pass <command>`, `ran-fail-fixed <command>`, `ran-fail-deferred <command> <issue|anchor>`, `not-detected`, `skipped <reason>`) and the five completion-report category labels (`durable`, `external-writes`, `test-only`, `verification`, `unverified-future`) move from skills/public/impl/references/verification-ladder.md lines 83-87 and 115-131 into the impl core's Closeout Vocabulary block as a terse token list (label + one-clause meaning, no worked examples). verification-ladder.md KEEPS the surrounding prose: the capability-seeking rule, browser/runtime/provider-API depth, the 'why separate the categories' rationale, the sibling-pointer routing. The Output Shape field names (`Implemented`, `Capability Delivered`, `Lint Gate`, `Verification`, `Unverified future behavior`, etc.) are already inline (SKILL.md line 154-161) and stay inline — they are the canonical example that this rule generalizes. Rule of thumb encoded in create-skill: a token is anything a run must reproduce verbatim to produce a well-formed or validator-passing closeout; everything that merely teaches WHEN/WHY is prose and stays in DEPTH refs.
proposals.headroom_resolution: Resolved by EXEMPTION, not by raising the cap. In scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py, `PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS` (line 30) already removes named H2 sections from the core_nonempty count via `_remove_pressure_exempt_sections` (line 91-103) before `_core_nonempty_lines` (line 106-108) tallies. Adding `'Closeout Vocabulary'` to that set means the inline token block is invisible to: (a) the build_report headroom panel (`core_nonempty`, line 359), (b) the commit-boundary ratchet `scan_changed_skill_md` -> `evaluate_core_headroom` (line 194-215, 162-191), and (c) the broad-gate core-headroom test that shares CORE_NONEMPTY_HEADROOM_BUFFER. So moving impl's ~7 vocab lines from a ref INTO core costs ZERO core-nonempty headroom — the ratchet's 'move prose into references/ to restore headroom' guidance (line 229-232) never fires for vocabulary. The MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 hard total still counts those bytes (they are real lines), and NEAR_CAP_WARN_LINES=195 still warns near the total cap — that is correct: the total ceiling is a file-size guard, the core-nonempty gate is the decision-prose-density guard, and only the latter was creating the exile pressure. The exemption surgically relieves the density gate while keeping the size guard honest.
proposals.floor_fix: Two-layer fix matching how the floor actually bites. Layer 1 (the real teeth): in scripts/claim_fidelity_lib.py the rule `requiredCommandFragments must be engage-always declaredReferences` (line 137-140) is what forces verification-ladder.md to be opened — RCF=['verification-ladder.md'] requires the literal ref-filename in the command log, satisfied only by a Bash/Read that OPENS the doc (collectOpenedBasenames + parseReadCommandBasenames in build-skill-execution-observation.mjs). Once the vocab is inline, the spec should STOP requiring the ref-filename as a command fragment and instead require the load-bearing TOKENS the run must emit: set requiredSummaryFragments to a representative closeout token (e.g. one of the Lint Gate statuses or a completion-report category that the pinned slice forces) and drop verification-ladder.md from requiredCommandFragments. That checks the actual claim ('did the run produce honest categorized closeout vocabulary') instead of a proxy ('did the run re-open the doc'). requiredSummaryFragments already matches against finalAssistantText (the parent closeout, .mjs line 664) so emitted-inline tokens pass without any re-read. Layer 2 (coverage as advisory + DEPTH-only denominator): coverage is ALREADY advisory at the observation layer — outcome is decided solely by findings (RCF+RSF), .mjs line 666; coveragePart (line 668-671) is a reported summary string, never gates outcome. To make the denominator honest, declaredReferences for coverage reporting should count only DEPTH refs (on-demand / skill-inlined-context engage-always whose prose is the value), excluding refs whose ONLY load-bearing content was promoted inline. Mechanically: keep declaredReferences as the skill-local set for completeness but report coverage over `declared minus inlined-token-owner refs`, OR mark such refs claimRole:'tokens-inlined' so the coverage line stops counting a not-opened verification-ladder.md as 'missing' once its tokens live in core. The engagement axis (claim_fidelity_lib ENGAGEMENT_VALUES line 13) gains no new enum value if we lean on the existing claimRole free-field used in spec.json (skill-inlined-context / verification-owner).
proposals.validator_safety: The parsed tokens stay BOTH emittable AND checkable, which is the whole point. EMITTABLE: with the enum inline in `## Closeout Vocabulary`, the run reproduces `ran-pass`/`not-detected`/`durable`/`unverified-future` from always-loaded context — no re-read needed, so the run can emit them in its finalAssistantText closeout cheaply. CHECKABLE: the claim-fidelity matchers are pure substring (fragmentFindings, .mjs line 615-632, normalized lowercase includes) over the command log (requiredCommandFragments) and the parent summary (requiredSummaryFragments). Moving the source-of-truth for a token from a ref into core does NOT change matchability — a token in the summary still substring-matches. The migration must preserve the three validator couplings I verified: (1) claim_fidelity_lib line 122-125 phantom check — declaredReferences must still exist under references/; verification-ladder.md still exists (it keeps its prose), so no phantom-ref break. (2) line 137-140 RCF-engage-always — once we drop verification-ladder.md from RCF and move the assertion to RSF, this constraint is trivially satisfied (RCF can be empty? note _validate_string_list line 57-62 requires NON-empty RCF, so RCF must still carry at least one engage-always ref-filename OR the validator must be relaxed to allow empty RCF when RSF carries the claim — that relaxation is part of this approach's churn). (3) build-skill-execution-observation outcome stays findings-only, so promoting tokens inline cannot accidentally flip a pass to fail. Net: tokens move home, matchers keep matching, and the only validator that needs a code change is the non-empty-RCF guard in claim_fidelity_lib.
proposals.churn_estimate: Small-to-moderate, concentrated and mechanical. (1) scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py: one-line set addition to PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS (line 30) + its test in tests/quality_gates covering _remove_pressure_exempt_sections — ~10 lines incl. test. (2) skills/public/create-skill/SKILL.md: amend the token-home rule (around line 97-104 + the Closeout Schema Rule region) and add the `## Closeout Vocabulary` convention to references/portable-authoring.md — ~15-25 lines of authoring contract, and the propagate-to-adjacent-skills rule (line 168-171) means I must scan whether other public skills have the same strand. (3) skills/public/impl/SKILL.md + verification-ladder.md: add the inline `## Closeout Vocabulary` block (~7-10 lines), trim the now-duplicated enum/category definitions from the ref to pointers (keep prose) — net core grows ~8 lines (all exempt), ref shrinks slightly. Watch MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200: impl SKILL.md is ~191 lines today, so the inline block risks the total ceiling and NEAR_CAP_WARN at 195 — may force a real prose trim elsewhere in impl core (honest cost, not a workaround). (4) evals/cautilus/impl-claim-fidelity/spec.json: move the claim from requiredCommandFragments to requiredSummaryFragments, update the long _comment rationale, possibly add claimRole:'tokens-inlined'. (5) scripts/claim_fidelity_lib.py: relax the non-empty-RCF guard (line 57-62 path) to permit RSF-carried claims, + the validate_claim_fidelity_specs test (tests/quality_gates/test_claim_fidelity_specs.py line 116 asserts the RCF-engage-always message — that test changes). docs/public-skill-validation.md narrative gets a sentence on token-home. Plus mandatory plugin mirror sync (sync_root_plugin_manifests) for the public-skill edit, and a fresh-eye critique. Estimate ~6-8 files, most edits under 20 lines each; the spec/validator coupling is the part that demands care.
risks: The MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 total ceiling is NOT exempted (only core_nonempty is) — pulling vocab inline can push a near-cap skill like impl (~191 lines) over 200 / past NEAR_CAP_WARN at 195, forcing a real prose trim. If reviewers trim load-bearing decision prose to make room for vocab, the cure reintroduces the disease at the total-ceiling layer. Mitigation: the total ceiling SHOULD bite here — it is the honest signal that the core is genuinely full; the fix is trimming true redundancy, not exempting the total too.

Exemption abuse: a new headroom-exempt H2 is an attractive nuisance. Authors may relabel ordinary decision prose as `## Closeout Vocabulary` to dodge the density gate, hollowing out the very budget that keeps cores lean. Needs a validator that the Closeout Vocabulary block contains only token-shaped lines (short label + clause, no multi-sentence paragraphs), or skill_ergonomics flags a Closeout Vocabulary section that exceeds N lines / contains prose paragraphs.

Token drift / dual source of truth: once the enum lives in impl core AND a pointer remains in verification-ladder.md, the two can diverge (core says `ran-fail-deferred`, ref example still shows old wording). The ref must be reduced to a pure pointer-to-core for the token list, not a parallel definition — otherwise we trade one strand for a desync bug.

Relaxing the non-empty requiredCommandFragments guard (claim_fidelity_lib line 57-62) weakens a real invariant: some specs legitimately need a command-log claim (e.g. a script the run MUST execute), and an empty RCF that silently relies on RSF could let a spec assert nothing checkable about commands. Mitigation: require RCF-or-RSF non-empty (at least one claim channel populated) rather than dropping the non-empty check entirely.

Generalization cost: create-skill's propagation rule (line 168-171) means this is not just an impl fix — every evaluator-required skill (setup, debug, find-skills, gather, handoff, issue, spec per public-skill-validation.md line 188-198) may have its own stranded closeout vocab. Doing impl alone fixes the named contradiction but leaves the pattern half-applied; doing all of them is a much larger slice than the churn estimate above and risks scope creep.

Coverage-denominator change is cosmetic teeth: because outcome is already findings-only (.mjs line 666), making the coverage denominator DEPTH-only improves the honesty of the reported summary but does not by itself stop any failure — the actual relief comes entirely from moving the claim off requiredCommandFragments. If a reviewer believes the denominator change is what fixes the floor, they will under-edit the spec and the wasteful re-read pressure (RCF=ref-filename) survives.

The pinned-slice spec prompt (impl spec.json line 8) was authored to FORCE verification-ladder.md open; if we move the claim to a summary token, we must re-verify the pinned slice still genuinely exercises the closeout vocabulary (a run could now emit the token from core without ever doing real categorized closeout work), so the spec's representativeness must be re-baselined with a fresh capture, not assumed.

proposals.approach: TEMPLATE-HOME: give load-bearing closeout tokens a THIRD residence — a per-skill fill-in CLOSEOUT TEMPLATE artifact that the run CONSUMES and EMITS — so they live neither in SKILL.md prose (which the headroom gate evicts) nor stranded inside a deep reference (which the coverage floor would force a wasteful re-read to credit). SKILL.md stays lean and points to the template; the template carries the exact emittable vocabulary (e.g. impl's Lint Gate statuses ran-pass/ran-fail-fixed/ran-fail-deferred/not-detected/skipped and the five completion-report categories durable/external-writes/test-only/verification/unverified-future); the run opens the template once, fills it, and writes the filled copy into its closeout bundle. The reference keeps the WHY/depth; the template owns the WHAT-to-emit. Concretely for quality, the analogous template carries the Lint Gate / completion-report-equivalent scorecard vocabulary the closeout must state.
proposals.policy_summary: Amend docs/public-skill-validation.md and skills/public/create-skill/SKILL.md to declare a fourth skill-surface class alongside SKILL.md core / references / scripts: a `closeout-template` artifact (proposed path skills/public/<skill>/templates/closeout.md, or an existing references/ file flagged role: closeout-template in catalog.yaml). Authoring rule: any token a closeout MUST emit verbatim (status enums, report-category names, gate labels) is AUTHORED ONCE in the template; the reference may explain it but is not the canonical emission source; SKILL.md names the template path in its Output Shape, never inlines the enum. The catalog.yaml/index.md routing gains a `closeout-template` role distinct from `required-primer`/`on-demand` so find-skills and the planner route the run to fill-the-template rather than read-then-restate. This makes 'where do emittable tokens live' a stated authoring boundary instead of an emergent tug-of-war between the two gates.
proposals.token_home: Exact rule: a token is TEMPLATE-RESIDENT iff (1) the closeout/Output-Shape contract requires it emitted verbatim, AND (2) it is a fixed vocabulary item (enum value, category name, gate label), not free judgment. Such tokens live in templates/closeout.md (or a catalog-flagged closeout-template ref) as literal fill-in slots — e.g. `Lint Gate: <ran-pass|ran-fail-fixed|ran-fail-deferred|not-detected|skipped>` and `Completion Report category: <durable|external-writes|test-only|verification|unverified-future>`. SKILL.md core holds only the pointer (`closeout per templates/closeout.md`), so the enum bytes never count against core_nonempty (and would be exempt anyway only if parked under `## References`/`## Load-Bearing Anchors`, the two PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS — the template removes them from SKILL.md entirely). References keep prose-for-judgment (the WHY each status means what it means) but stop being the sole home of the emittable strings. Tokens that are genuine judgment (a written rationale paragraph) stay in references and are NOT migrated.
proposals.headroom_resolution: Resolved structurally, not by raising MAX_CORE_NONEMPTY_LINES (160) or the buffer (4). The enum/category vocabulary that would otherwise bloat SKILL.md core moves into templates/closeout.md, which is neither core nor counted: _core_nonempty_lines in check_skill_surface_preflight.py only measures the skill's own SKILL.md body minus the two exempt H2 sections; a templates/ file is a separate path the preflight treats under target_kind handling and never folds into core_nonempty. So core headroom strictly improves (or is neutral) — the gate's own remediation text ('Move prose into references/ or scripts/') is honored with a third sink that, unlike references, the run can EMIT from cheaply. No constant changes; the ratchet in scan_changed_skill_md keeps working because SKILL.md only loses lines.
proposals.floor_fix: The floor stops punishing non-re-read because filling the template IS an open-and-emit event the existing matchers already credit, with NO matcher code change required. (1) Coverage (collectOpenedBasenames, line 519): when the run reads templates/closeout.md once and writes the filled copy into its bundle, both the Read tool_use (file_path key, FILE_PATH_KEYS) and any `cat`/`sed`/Write of the template register its basename as opened — coverage credit without a deep-ref re-read. (2) The actual blocking floor is requiredCommandFragments (line 663), which substring-matches the FULL command log: point each skill's declared floor fragment at the template basename (e.g. requiredCommandFragments: ["closeout.md"] or the specific template name) so the floor is satisfied by the template-fill the run already does, not by forcing a re-open of verification-ladder.md. (3) Emittability stays via requiredSummaryFragments: the filled enum tokens land in the final summary, so a spec can assert ["ran-pass", ...] or category names directly against finalAssistantText. The contradiction dissolves because the floor's required artifact (template) is the SAME artifact the run must emit from, so 'don't re-read what you already have' and 'prove you engaged the canonical source' become one action. declaredReferences (the advisory coverage list) can keep listing the deep references for honesty without making them the blocking floor — coverage is advisory (outcome derives from findings only, line 666), so the deep refs missing from `opened` degrades the coverage line but does not fail the run.
proposals.validator_safety: All three parsed-token surfaces stay emittable and checkable with zero matcher rewrites. EMITTABLE: the run fills templates/closeout.md, writes it into the bundle, and restates the chosen enum values in its parent summary, so literal tokens appear in (a) the command log (template path + any sed/cat/Write of it), (b) the written bundle file, and (c) finalAssistantText. CHECKABLE: requiredCommandFragments still substring-matches the full command log (catches the template basename and the fill fragments); requiredSummaryFragments still substring-matches the parent final summary (catches emitted enum/category strings); declaredReferences coverage runs unchanged as the advisory secondary metric. The impl spec (evals/cautilus/impl-claim-fidelity/spec.json) migrates by pointing requiredCommandFragments at the template basename instead of ['verification-ladder.md'] AND adding requiredSummaryFragments for the actual closeout vocabulary — strictly stronger, since today the floor only proves the file was NAMED in a command whereas the template-fill proves the vocabulary was EMITTED. PASS/FAIL semantics (outcome from findings only, line 666) are untouched so no eval flips on coverage pressure. Bounded risk: requiredCommandFragments is a blunt 'named anywhere' matcher, so the template basename must be distinctive and skill-scoped (closeout.md under the skill dir is fine; a repo-wide generic name would cross-credit between skills).
proposals.churn_estimate: Per migrated skill (impl first, then quality): +1 template file (~15-30 lines, templates/closeout.md); move the enum/category vocabulary out of the companion reference into the template, leaving the WHY prose (impl SKILL.md core change ~0 — it already only points at verification-ladder.md, the win is no forced re-read to emit); +1 catalog.yaml row (role: closeout-template) and +1 index.md mention; edit the Output Shape line in SKILL.md to name the template path (1 line, no core growth). Per eval spec: ~6 changed lines (swap requiredCommandFragments target, add requiredSummaryFragments enum list, add template to declaredReferences). Policy docs: ~1 paragraph in docs/public-skill-validation.md (new surface class) and ~1 in create-skill/SKILL.md authoring contract. Code: ZERO changes to check_skill_surface_preflight.py and ZERO to build-skill-execution-observation.mjs — existing FILE_PATH_KEYS + READ_COMMANDS + requiredCommandFragments machinery already credits template fills. First slice total (impl + policy + spec): ~3 files added, ~5 edited, no validator logic touched.
risks: Template-vs-reference drift: the emittable enum now lives in the template while the WHY lives in the reference; if they fall out of sync (a status renamed in one, not the other) the run emits a stale token. Mitigation: a cheap deterministic gate asserting every status/category token in the template appears in its companion reference (or vice versa), reusing the fragment-match philosophy; otherwise this just relocates the duplication problem.

Floor blunting: requiredCommandFragments matches the basename ANYWHERE in the command log, so a run that merely `ls templates/` or greps the template without filling it would falsely satisfy the floor. The template-fill is only genuinely proven by requiredSummaryFragments emitting the actual enum values, so the floor MUST move its teeth to summary fragments, not stay on the command fragment alone — otherwise the floor is weaker than today's 'opened verification-ladder.md'.

Coverage metric hollowing: moving the canonical vocabulary out of declared references means a run can now legitimately NOT open the deep reference and still close honestly; the advisory coverage line will show lower declared-ref coverage. This is correct (the re-read was the waste) but it removes a signal reviewers may have leaned on — the policy doc must state that closeout-template fill is the new evidence, so a low coverage number on a closeout-template skill is not read as a regression.

Surface proliferation: a fourth skill-surface class (templates/) adds authoring and routing burden; create-skill, catalog.yaml schema, find-skills, and the planner all must learn the new role. If only one or two skills ever need an emittable-vocabulary template, the policy weight may exceed the benefit versus simply tagging an existing reference as closeout-template (the lighter variant — prefer it unless a true fill-in template earns its keep).

Validator-safety edge: if the template basename collides with another skill's template (generic name) the requiredCommandFragments floor cross-credits across skills, silently passing a run that filled the WRONG skill's template. Must enforce skill-scoped, distinctive template basenames at author time.

Emission ordering: collectOpenedBasenames and the summary matcher read the PARENT track's final text (finalAssistantText skips isSidechain); if a subagent fills the template but the parent never restates the chosen enums, requiredSummaryFragments fails even though the work happened. The contract must require the parent closeout to restate the filled tokens, matching how impl already surfaces Lint Gate in the parent summary.

proposals.approach: MEASUREMENT-ONLY. Do not relocate a single token. Resolve the contradiction by denying that most of it is a contradiction, and fix the one place that actually lies: the reference-coverage measurement. Three coordinated edits — (1) make coverage advisory with a DEPTH-only denominator, (2) add a per-ref CLASS TAG (DUP / INLINE / DEPTH) carried in the spec referenceEngagement and surfaced in the catalog, (3) reclassify closeout-vocabulary refs (e.g. impl/verification-ladder.md) as DEPTH-on-closeout — a legitimate must-read-AT-the-closeout-trigger, not a passive open-count miss. The headroom gate stays exactly as written. The token home stays exactly where it is. Grounded entirely in real files: scripts/check_python_lengths.py (the advisory INTERPRETATION + warn-band philosophy I mirror), scripts/check_skill_surface_preflight.py + scripts/staged_commit_gate_plan_helpers.py (the unchanged core-headroom ratchet), scripts/agent-runtime/build-skill-execution-observation.mjs lines 658-660/668-671/737 (the coverage denominator I narrow), evals/cautilus/impl-claim-fidelity/spec.json (the closeout-vocab ref I reclassify), scripts/claim_fidelity_lib.py + the methodology contract charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md lines 110-124 (the claimRole field I extend into a class tag).
proposals.policy_summary: The headroom gate and the coverage floor are NOT in genuine conflict for most refs — they measure different things and the conflict is an artifact of one flat denominator. check_skill_surface_preflight.py (MAX_CORE_NONEMPTY_LINES ratchet at the commit boundary via staged_commit_gate_plan_helpers.skill_core_headroom_gates) rewards moving SCHEMAS/EXAMPLES/THEORY out of SKILL.md core into references/ — this is create-skill SKILL.md lines 101-106 doing its job. The coverage measurement in build-skill-execution-observation.mjs counts opened/declared over ALL declaredReferences (each spec's _comment literally says 'declaredReferences is the full set (coverage)'). The fix is to classify WHY each ref exists and credit the right runtime behavior per class. Policy: (a) DUP = a ref whose load-bearing tokens duplicate what core already says — the run already has the intent and a faithful run must NOT re-read it; coverage must never punish a DUP miss. (b) INLINE = the methodology contract's existing claimRole 'skill-inlined-context' (used 5x today) — gist lives in core, doc holds slice-specific depth opened only on a narrower trigger; engage-always for the concern but NOT a coverage floor. (c) DEPTH = unique content a representative run is genuinely forced to open, the only legitimate coverage-floor population; a closeout-vocab ref read AT the closeout trigger is honest DEPTH, not a miss. This is a strict extension of the locked methodology contract (lines 120-121 already permit claimRole to be 'another local note used only by the harness'), so no contract is overturned — it is sharpened.
proposals.token_home: Exact rule: tokens stay where the headroom gate already put them. The token-home rule is unchanged by design — that is the whole point of MEASUREMENT-ONLY. A load-bearing vocabulary string (e.g. the Lint Gate status vocabulary ran-pass/ran-fail-fixed/ran-fail-deferred/not-detected/skipped and the five completion-report categories that live ONLY in impl/references/verification-ladder.md per its spec) stays in references/ where create-skill SKILL.md line 106 ('move schemas, examples, and theory into references/') and the core-headroom ratchet both want it. We do NOT pull it back into SKILL.md core to satisfy coverage, because the run that needs it opens it exactly when the closeout trigger fires — and the new measurement credits that open as DEPTH-on-closeout. The decision procedure for where a token lives is single-owner: the headroom gate decides, coverage never argues with it. Coverage's only job becomes 'did the run open the DEPTH refs its trigger reached', which is satisfiable WITHOUT relocation. Net: token home = current location, frozen; the stranding disappears because we stop scoring relocation as a miss.
proposals.headroom_resolution: Headroom is resolved by leaving it untouched and removing the counter-pressure from the other side. check_skill_surface_preflight.py evaluate_core_headroom (MAX_CORE_NONEMPTY_LINES, the >=buffer ratchet) and its commit-boundary wiring in staged_commit_gate_plan_helpers.skill_core_headroom_gates stay byte-for-byte identical — no new constant, no exemption, no band change. The headroom gate keeps pushing vocab OUT. What changes is that OUT is no longer punished IN: today a vocab ref moved out to satisfy headroom then shows up in the coverage denominator and a faithful run that doesn't wastefully re-read it scores as a miss. After the fix, that ref is tagged DEPTH-on-closeout (forced only at the trigger) or INLINE/DUP (never a floor), so headroom-driven relocation no longer creates a coverage debt. The two gates stop fighting not because headroom moved but because coverage stopped scoring the thing headroom is allowed to do. This mirrors the existing precedent in check_python_lengths.py: the hard length limit (verified fact) and the advisory warn-band/INTERPRETATION (inference layer) coexist by being explicitly different trust tiers — I apply the same two-tier split to coverage.
proposals.floor_fix: Mechanics, minimal and concrete. (1) build-skill-execution-observation.mjs: change the denominator at lines 658-660. Today: declared = spec.declaredReferences; covered = declared.filter(opened); missing = declared.filter(!opened). New: derive a depthRefs subset from spec.referenceEngagement where the per-ref classTag === 'DEPTH' (and engagement is reached by the run's trigger). Coverage numerator/denominator computes over depthRefs only; DUP and INLINE refs are reported in a separate advisory line, never in the floor ratio. The full declared list is still emitted (for the validator and for backward parse-compat) but as report.coverage.declaredAll; report.coverage.declared becomes depthRefs.length. (2) Make the coverage LINE advisory in tone exactly like check_python_lengths.py's ADVISORY:/INTERPRETATION pattern — coverage already does not drive outcome (only fragmentFindings does, line 666), so this is honest labeling, not a behavior change to pass/fail. (3) spec edits: add 'classTag' (DUP|INLINE|DEPTH) beside the existing engagement/rationale/claimRole in each referenceEngagement entry — claimRole already carries free-text local notes so this slots in. For impl/spec.json, verification-ladder.md gets classTag DEPTH with rationale 'closeout vocabulary forced AT the closeout trigger' and the four skill-inlined refs get classTag INLINE (matching their existing claimRole skill-inlined-context). (4) claim_fidelity_lib.py _validate_engagement: add optional classTag validation (one of the three values when present) — additive, defaults tolerant so un-tagged specs still pass. (5) catalog.yaml (quality) and index.md: surface the class tag in the routing role line so a maintainer browsing sees DUP/INLINE/DEPTH. The floor that 'pulls reading IN' now pulls IN only DEPTH refs the run's own trigger reached — which is exactly what an honest run already does.
proposals.validator_safety: The parsed tokens in issue/impl/quality stay BOTH emittable and checkable. Emittable: nothing moves, so requiredCommandFragments (e.g. 'verification-ladder.md' in impl/spec.json) still appears in the command log when the run opens the doc at the closeout trigger — DEPTH-on-closeout is precisely the case where the run DOES open it, so the fragment matcher in fragmentFindings (lines 615-632, the only thing driving outcome) keeps firing. Checkable: claim_fidelity_lib.validate_spec still enforces that requiredCommandFragments are engage-always declaredReferences (line 137-140) — a DEPTH ref must be engage-always to be a required fragment, which holds: DEPTH-on-closeout IS engage-always (every representative run reaches closeout). The validator's existing invariants (declaredReferences = full references/ set, every declared ref has an engagement entry, on-demand needs trigger) are UNTOUCHED; classTag is additive-optional. Backward-compat is preserved per the methodology contract's hard requirement (lines 127-131): declaredReferences stays a string list, the builder still parses it, and report.coverage.declaredAll preserves the old full count for any consumer. The mjs tests (tests/agent-runtime/build-skill-execution-observation.test.mjs lines 217-330, asserting coverage.covered/declared/coveredRefs/missingRefs) need updating to the depthRefs denominator and the new declaredAll field — that is the load-bearing test churn and it is in-scope, not a regression.
proposals.churn_estimate: Small-to-moderate, concentrated. CODE: ~30-45 lines net in build-skill-execution-observation.mjs (denominator split + advisory line + report shape: declared->depthRefs, add declaredAll); ~8-12 lines in scripts/claim_fidelity_lib.py (_validate_engagement optional classTag). SPECS: 25 claim-fidelity spec.json files get a classTag added per referenceEngagement entry — mechanical, ~1 line per ref, but spread across 25 files (the largest churn surface; 20 already carry claimRole so the editing pattern is established). The reclassification narrative (DEPTH-on-closeout) needs a one-sentence rationale touch in the handful of specs with closeout-vocab refs (impl, issue, quality, release — the skills whose refs hold closeout/completion vocabulary). DOCS: methodology contract gets a ~15-line 'Class Tag' subsection under the existing Engagement Tag Contract (lines 85-131); quality catalog.yaml + index.md get the tag surfaced (~10 lines). TESTS: build-skill-execution-observation.test.mjs coverage assertions rewritten to depthRefs denominator (~5 test blocks, lines 217-330 region) + 1-2 new tests proving a DUP/INLINE miss does NOT lower the floor. No new scripts, no new gates, no relocation diffs. Total touched files ~30, but most are 1-line spec additions; the genuine engineering is one .mjs function and its tests.
risks: Spec authoring burden and drift: 25 specs now carry a third axis (classTag) on top of engagement+claimRole; if authors mis-tag a genuine DEPTH ref as DUP to dodge a coverage miss, the floor silently weakens. Mitigation: claim_fidelity_lib validation can require a rationale sentence on any DUP tag, but it cannot judge truthfulness — this is the honest soft spot of measurement-only.

DEPTH-on-closeout can be gamed the same way the methodology contract already warns about fakeability (the retro expert-lens.md failure documented in the hitl spec _comment): if the closeout vocabulary is reconstructible from general knowledge or from the SKILL.md preview, a run can emit a plausible closeout WITHOUT opening the DEPTH ref, and the fragment matcher (which only checks the basename string appears in the command log) would then pass on a re-read-free run only if the run actually cat'd the file. Reclassifying as DEPTH assumes the vocab is genuinely repo-specific and non-fabricable; if it is not, the floor is theater. This must be verified per-ref, not assumed.

Measurement-only does nothing for the case where the token home IS genuinely wrong — if a load-bearing string was forced OUT of core by headroom but the run needs it BEFORE any trigger fires (not at closeout), no class tag fixes that; the run still must wastefully open the ref early. This approach declares such cases out of scope by asserting they are rare, but it cannot prove they are rare without the live captures the cautilus contract gates behind ask-before-run.

Backward-compat surface: report.coverage.declared changes meaning (full set -> depthRefs subset). Any external consumer reading that field as 'total references' breaks. run_skill_efficiency_ab.py and the A/B aggregation read coverage; declaredAll mitigates but only if every consumer is migrated — an un-audited reader is a latent bug.

The contradiction is resolved by re-labeling, not by reducing the number of refs a run touches. A skeptic can argue we renamed the problem: the run still must open DEPTH refs at closeout, and if closeout vocab is large across many skills, the aggregate read cost is unchanged — we made the measurement honest about it but did not make the run cheaper. This approach explicitly accepts that trade: honesty over relocation.

Catalog/index and the methodology contract now encode a three-value taxonomy that must stay in sync with the spec field and the .mjs consumer across mutate->sync->verify; a partial edit (tag in specs but not surfaced in catalog, or validated in lib but not consumed in mjs) is exactly the generated-surface drift the repo phase rules warn against.

per_skill_plans.risk_notes:
SETUP IS NOT IMPL — the keystone's '## Closeout Vocabulary' core-promotion machinery does NOT apply here, and forcing it would be a mistake. setup has zero validator-checked closeout ENUMS that a run must emit verbatim from SKILL.md core (both specs have requiredSummaryFragments=[]). All four 'INLINE' refs are actually DEPTH refs (unique teaching/checklist prose + script-emitted vocabulary), not stranded emittable tokens. The ONLY genuine teeth is RCF=ref-filename re-read pinning (greenfield-flow.md / normalization-flow.md / agent-docs-policy.md / default-surfaces.md in requiredCommandFragments), which is exactly Move C's target.

LOUD FLAG #1 (validator-checked tokens that look strandable but are NOT in the refs): the '## Subagent Delegation' substrings — 'explicit user delegation request', 'already delegated', 'second user message', 'host blocks', 'same-agent pass', and the compact-form 'same-agent substitutes are forbidden' / 'no same-agent' / 'do not substitute a same-agent' / 'same-agent pass fails' — ARE hard-enforced by scripts/setup_agent_docs_fresh_eye_lib.py (FRESH_EYE_REQUIRED_SNIPPETS lines 8-13, FRESH_EYE_COMPACT_REQUIRED_SNIPPETS lines 15-28) against the TARGET repo's AGENTS.md, and the canonical copy-paste lives in scripts/templates/agents_subagent_delegation.txt. DO NOT 'lift' these out of agent-docs-policy.md into SKILL.md and DO NOT delete them from the ref under a coverage-collapse assumption: the ref's verbatim template (lines 97-120 + compact 131-138) must stay byte-aligned with the script template and the lib's required snippets. If a reviewer trims the ref's template, the ref/script/lib trio drifts and a downstream consumer repo's AGENTS.md can silently lose an inspector-required substring (#258-class generated-surface drift). Token home is already correct (script template); leave it.

LOUD FLAG #2 (RCF floor relaxation needs a real capture, not an assumption): greenfield.spec.json is NOT capturable inside the mature charness repo (its own _comment lines 2 says running /charness:setup here detects PARTIAL/NORMALIZE and never reaches greenfield-flow.md). So you CANNOT honestly re-baseline a greenfield requiredSummaryFragments token in-repo. Per dissent #3, do NOT drop greenfield-flow.md from greenfield RCF on an assumed token — either (a) capture in a fresh/empty sandbox first (ask-before-run Cautilus), or (b) leave greenfield-flow.md in RCF and only add the advisory classTag this slice. normalization.spec.json IS in-repo-capturable, so its RCF->RSF move is feasible with a real capture. Applying Move C asymmetrically (normalization captured, greenfield deferred) is correct and honest.

LOUD FLAG #3 (default-surfaces.md may legitimately KEEP its RCF pin): there is no single emittable RSF token that proves 'consulted the multi-surface scaffold checklist'; 'opened default-surfaces.md' is a faithful proxy. Unlike impl's verification-ladder.md (one ref-filename standing in for an emittable enum), default-surfaces.md re-read is not wasteful — it is the surface-set work itself. Flag to the fresh-eye reviewer: keep default-surfaces.md in RCF unless a captured run shows a clean surface-set RSF token. Blanket-applying 'drop every RCF ref-filename' here would over-relax the floor (the disease the keystone's dissent #2 warns about, in mirror form).

LOUD FLAG #4 (classTag taxonomy sync): if the DEPTH/INLINE/DUP classTag lands, it MUST be added coherently across spec field (both setup specs) + claim_fidelity_lib._validate_engagement + build-skill-execution-observation.mjs consumer + quality catalog — a partial edit is the generated-surface drift the phase rules call out. Three keystone infrastructure changes must ship before gather's spec.json can be edited: (1) 'Closeout Vocabulary' added to PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS (check_skill_surface_preflight.py line 30); (2) claim_fidelity_lib._validate_string_list relaxed from 'requiredCommandFragments must be non-empty' to 'at least one of RCF/RSF non-empty' (line 57-62 / 137) — WITHOUT this, dropping capability-contract.md from gather's RCF is IMPOSSIBLE because the validator hard-requires RCF non-empty AND every RCF entry engage-always; (3) optional classTag support in _validate_engagement. Per the keystone dissent #4, impl is the proving slice and the rest (gather included) should be a FILED ISSUE sweep, NOT scope-crept into the impl change. So this gather contract is the DESIGN for that sweep, not an immediately-appliable edit.

VALIDATOR-CHECKED TOKEN RISK (flag loudly): gather's requiredCommandFragments is ['source-priority.md','capability-contract.md'] — BOTH are currently validator-enforced RCF floors (validator_enforced=true). The capability-contract.md Access Mode enum being lifted to core is exactly the kind of emittable closeout vocabulary the keystone warns about. Two distinct dangers: (a) If capability-contract.md is dropped from RCF but the requiredSummaryFragments token is NOT re-baselined from a real capture (keystone dissent #3), the floor could pass on a run that emits 'public' from always-loaded core WITHOUT doing real access-mode judgment — i.e. the prompt (spec.json line 8) was authored to force the doc open; a summary-token check is a weaker proxy unless the pinned slice genuinely forces categorized access-mode work. Re-verify representativeness with a FRESH Cautilus capture (ask-before-run). (b) source-priority.md carries NO liftable token, so it must REMAIN an enforced floor (RCF or a faithful re-read trigger) — do not drop it just because capability-contract.md moves; that would silently weaken the primary-source-identity teeth gather exists to protect.

DRIFT RISK: After lifting the Access Mode enum, capability-contract.md's '## Access Modes' block (lines 22-39) MUST be reduced to a pointer-to-core, not left as a parallel definition — a dual source of the six labels is exactly the drift the keystone token-home rule eliminates. The enum also appears (correctly, as judgment prose, not a parallel canonical list) in SKILL.md Workflow step 3 (lines 67-72, the acquisition ladder) and Guardrails; those are usage-context mentions, not a competing definition, so leave them.

CROSS-SURFACE SYNC: The classTag taxonomy must stay in sync across mutate->sync->verify for gather — spec.json referenceEngagement field, claim_fidelity_lib validator, build-skill-execution-observation.mjs consumer, and quality catalog/index routing lines. A partial edit (e.g. classTag in spec.json but lib doesn't accept it) is the generated-surface drift the phase rules warn against. The plugin mirror (sync_root_plugin_manifests) must run for the gather SKILL.md + capability-contract.md edits before any validator pass.

NON-LIFT CONFIRMATION: source-priority.md, adapter-contract.md, asset-refresh.md carry NO emittable-verbatim closeout token (only teaching/recipe/schema prose already gisted in SKILL.md), so they correctly stay DEPTH references with their files unchanged. Promoting any of their prose to core would burn real file-size headroom (the size guard is intentionally NOT exempt) for content that is not a verbatim-emit requirement — that is the keystone's rejected disease, not the cure.

FRESH-EYE CLOSEOUT: per the repo contract, the gather edit (when it lands as part of the sweep) is task-completing repo work and requires a fresh-eye bounded critique in the shared parent worktree (read-only base inspection via git show; never index/worktree-mutating git ops).

verdicts.reason: Confirmed against scripts/claim_fidelity_lib.py: the spec.json half of this lift BREAKS the deterministic claim-fidelity validator if applied before the keystone lib relaxation lands. (1) _validate_string_list (lines 57-62) hard-requires requiredCommandFragments to be a NON-EMPTY list, and lines 137-140 require every RCF entry to be engage-always — verified by test_required_command_fragment_must_be_engage_always. gather's RCF today is ['source-priority.md','capability-contract.md'] and both validate as engage-always (confirmed: validate_registry returns engage_always=['adapter-contract.md','capability-contract.md','source-priority.md']). Dropping capability-contract.md leaves RCF=['source-priority.md'], which is still non-empty and still engage-always, so that single drop does NOT itself break the non-empty guard — BUT the plan's stated intent (move the floor off the ref-filename onto a requiredSummaryFragments token) is the RCF-or-RSF relaxation the lib does NOT yet support; there is no classTag handling in _validate_engagement (lines 42-54 read only engagement/rationale/trigger/gate) and no RCF-or-RSF logic. The plan EXPLICITLY defers all of this on the keystone shipping first, and flags the token-re-baseline danger (a summary-token check is a weaker proxy than forcing the doc open; the pinned python-docs slice is NOT confirmed to force 'public', so the requiredSummaryFragments token must be re-baselined from a FRESH ask-before-run Cautilus capture — not assumed). Until the keystone lib relaxation + classTag support land and the token is re-baselined from a real capture, applying the spec.json edit risks (a) validator rejection if RCF is touched in a way the current lib forbids, and (b) a silently-weakened floor that passes on always-loaded-core 'public' without real access-mode judgment. The headroom half is fine (current core_nonempty 103/160 = 57 left, total 147/200 = 53 left; +~7 lines stays far under both limits even WITHOUT the PRESSURE_EXEMPT exemption — the exemption is harmless belt-and-suspenders, not load-bearing), and the ref-pointer reduction kills dual-source drift correctly. But because the validator-enforced floor is in play and the safe path is strictly gated on infrastructure that has not shipped, this is safe=false per the default-on-uncertainty rule and the plan's own loud sequencing warning.

SCOPE CORRECTION (load-bearing): create-skill has ZERO deletes and ZERO actual token lifts. All 6 'INLINE' refs are classTag-INLINE/DEPTH advisory-coverage cases, NOT token-home lifts. A reviewer who reads 'INLINE' as 'lift into core' would WRONGLY manufacture a Closeout Vocabulary block create-skill does not need and would bloat its 189-line core toward the 195/200 ceiling for nothing.

NO VALIDATOR-CHECKED EMITTABLE TOKEN IS STRANDED. So create-skill's 4 RCF refs stay RCF (still forcing a doc-open re-read) BY DESIGN this slice. Do not 'fix' create-skill's RCF in this change — that is the filed sweep.

classTag IS COSMETIC-ONLY for create-skill (cannot change outcome). slices.rationale: impl is the keystone's explicit proving slice — the one place the RCF=ref-filename re-read pressure is genuinely relieved. It is validator-enforced and the highest-risk single change (HARD 200-line ceiling knife-edge, empty-RCF-fails-closed unless the order is exactly relax-guard -> add-RSF -> drop-RCF, and a real Cautilus re-baseline is required because the prompt was authored to FORCE the doc open). It gets its OWN carefully-gated slice and must follow Slice 1. State in bold in the PR: Move C Part 1 (RCF->RSF) is load-bearing; classTag/denominator is honesty-only. The ask-before-run capture gates the RSF token choice — do not assume the token.

slices.rationale: Per keystone dissent #4: impl is the proving slice; sweeping the rest is issue-filed, NOT scope-crept into the proving change. Every item here is a validator-enforced floor move that REQUIRES a fresh Cautilus capture to choose an honest RSF token (a summary token can pass on always-loaded core without real work — dissent #3). Grouping them under one tracked issue, each landed as its own gate-clean slice after the pattern is proven, keeps each independently reviewable while preventing a half-plumbed floor. greenfield-flow.md and default-surfaces.md are explicitly flagged to STAY RCF-pinned (one not in-repo-capturable, one a faithful multi-surface proxy) — applying Move C blindly there would over-relax a healthy floor. issue and markdown-preview need NO change (no stranded token, no actionable surface) and are intentionally absent.

execution.ordering_rationale: Strict dependency layering. Slice 1 is forced first by three grounded facts verified against the repo: classTag exists in ZERO consumers (claim_fidelity_lib, build-skill-execution-observation.mjs, both tests), PRESSURE_EXEMPT_H2_SECTIONS contains only {Load-Bearing Anchors, References}, and _validate_string_list HARD-rejects an empty requiredCommandFragments list (line 57-62, invoked at 137). So no per-skill classTag write, headroom-exempt vocab block, or RCF->RSF move can validate until the mechanism + the create-skill authoring rule it follows exist. Slices 2-3 are next because DEAD/pure-DUP deletes are the lowest-risk class (no eval-spec coupling, no headroom interaction, fully deterministic, no fresh capture) and the spec deletes buy total-line headroom the later spec lift needs. Slice 4 batches the genuinely safe advisory-only classTag annotations (cosmetic, additive, tolerant-default, no floor move) once the validator accepts the field. Slice 5 is the impl PROVING SLICE — the single highest-risk validator-enforced change (200-line knife-edge, empty-RCF-fails-closed, ask-before-run capture required) gets its own carefully-gated slice and demonstrates the RCF->RSF pattern. Slice 6 is the one non-floor spec enum lift, low-risk because acceptance-checks.md was never an RCF (no capture). Slice 7 is the deferred, issue-filed sweep of every remaining floor rewrite, each gated on a fresh Cautilus capture and landed as its own reviewable sub-slice. This honors the constraints: DEAD+DUP before INLINE lifts; validator-enforced lifts (impl, then critique/hitl/etc.) in their own gated slices; and never a floor/gate change mixed with a mass doc edit (spec deletes split from spec lift; impl floor move isolated).
execution.total_files_estimate: Roughly 70-95 files across all slices. Slice 1: ~7-9 (check_skill_surface_preflight.py, claim_fidelity_lib.py, build-skill-execution-observation.mjs, run_skill_efficiency_ab.py, the .test.mjs, create-skill SKILL.md + portable-authoring.md, 2 docs, plus mirror). Slice 2: ~9-12 (3 ref files + 3 SKILL.md + mirrors + find-skills/latest.json). Slice 3: ~11-13 (8 ref files + spec SKILL.md + spec.json + mirror). Slice 4: ~7-9 spec.json files + mirrors (advisory only). Slice 5: ~6-8 (impl SKILL.md, verification-ladder.md, impl spec.json + mirror + test files). Slice 6: ~4-5 (spec SKILL.md, acceptance-checks.md, spec.json + mirror). Slice 7 (deferred sweep, multiple sub-slices): ~26-39 across 8 skills (each ~3-5 surfaces: SKILL.md + ref + 1-3 spec.json + mirror).
biggest_risks: EMPTY-RCF FAILS CLOSED: dropping the only requiredCommandFragments entry (impl Slice 5; hotl Slice 7) before Slice 1's RCF-or-RSF guard relaxation lands raises 'requiredCommandFragments must be a non-empty string list' and red-fails the whole claim-fidelity registry gate. Order is non-negotiable: relax guard (Slice 1) -> add RSF token -> drop RCF entry, atomically.

200-LINE TOTAL CEILING KNIFE-EDGE: the Closeout Vocabulary exemption relieves ONLY core-nonempty density, NOT MAX_SKILL_MD_LINES=200 (validate_skills.py line 351). impl 190+9=~199 (zero margin), spec 194, critique 194, find-skills 198 are all near/over the cap. The fix is trimming TRUE redundancy, never trimming load-bearing decision prose and never exempting the total — otherwise the cure reintroduces the disease at the size layer.

HOLLOW RSF TOKEN: every RCF->RSF move risks a run emitting the summary token from always-loaded core WITHOUT doing real categorized work (the prompts were authored to FORCE the doc open). Each requiredSummaryFragments token MUST be re-baselined from a FRESH ask-before-run Cautilus capture, never assumed; 'not-detected' is a trap for impl because survey_verification.py emits it literally.

GENERATED-SURFACE / TAXONOMY DRIFT: classTag must stay synced across spec.json field + claim_fidelity_lib._validate_engagement + build-skill-execution-observation.mjs consumer + quality catalog; a classTag written before the lib accepts it, or a denominator change without the .test.mjs migration, is exactly the mutate->sync->verify drift the phase rules forbid. Plus every public-skill edit needs sync_root_plugin_manifests before verify or check_staged_mirror_drift fails.

OVER-ZEALOUS SWEEP eroding healthy floors: design-lenses.md/evidence-durability.md (spec), debug-memory.md (debug), greenfield-flow.md + default-surfaces.md (setup), source-priority.md (gather) are RCF floors that must NOT be deleted or RCF-dropped — some are genuine forced reads, not wasteful re-read proxies. A reviewer mechanically applying the impl recipe would silently weaken teeth no validator catches.

MISLABELED plan_input flags: validator_enforced=false is WRONG for critique, hitl, find-skills, and debug — their artifact/routing validators DO substring-match enums in reference files. Trusting the flag and lift-deleting those refs strands validator-checked tokens and red-fails the gate.
execution.rollback_note: Each slice is an independent commit (or small commit group) and is revertible in isolation because the slicing respects the dependency DAG: later slices only ADD on top of Slice 1's mechanism. To roll back, git revert in reverse dependency order. Slice 1 is the only slice that, if reverted, forces reverting everything above it (it owns the validator/exemption/denominator the rest depend on) — so revert Slices 7->6->5->4->3->2 first, then Slice 1 last. Slices 2 and 3 (deletes) are independently revertible by restoring the deleted ref files + their SKILL.md bullets + spec.json entries together (the bidirectional inventory gate means file and listing must be restored as a pair). Slice 4 (advisory classTag) is trivially revertible — removing the additive fields cannot break anything since they are tolerant-default. Slices 5/6 (lifts) revert by restoring the trimmed reference sections from git show <base>:<path> and reverting the spec.json RCF/RSF fields together. The cross-cutting safety rule: never leave a spec.json with empty requiredCommandFragments AND empty requiredSummaryFragments after a partial revert — that fails the RCF-or-RSF guard; restore the RCF entry in the same revert. Run scripts/sync_root_plugin_manifests.py + check_staged_mirror_drift.py after every revert that touched a public skill, and re-run test_claim_fidelity_specs.py + the .test.mjs as the post-revert green check.
